qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL comment mutation bypasses unpublished proposal authorization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an authorization bypass in the GraphQL `commentable(...){ addComment(...) }` mutation for unpublished proposals; the advisory root cause is unsanitized user-name rendering on comment/log pages, fixed by sanitizing presenter output rather than changing GraphQL authorization.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's stored XSS root cause. GHSA-fc46-r95f-hq7g is a username-rendering sanitization bug in Decidim comment/log presentation code, while the session's only final qualifying finding is a separate GraphQL unpublished-proposal authorization bypass.
evidence: The advisory describes stored code execution through the user name field when a victim passively visits a comment page.

The Decidim fix in releases v0.30.5 and v0.31.1 changes `decidim-core/app/presenters/decidim/user_presenter.rb` and `decidim-core/app/presenters/decidim/log/user_presenter.rb` to sanitize rendered user names with `decidim_sanitize_translated` instead of returning raw HTML-safe content.

The `graphql_unpublished_proposal_comment_bypass` finding instead relies on `Decidim::Comments::MutationExtensions#commentable` resolving unpublished proposals and `CommentableMutationType#add_comment` persisting a comment without the proposal publication guard.
